description_long: This is an electric guitar with a distinctive V-shaped body, reminiscent of the Gibson Flying V design. The body features a two-tone color scheme, with a cream or light-colored pickguard contrasting against a predominantly black finish. The guitar has two humbucker pickups, a three-way toggle switch, and three control knobs, all mounted on the pickguard. The neck is long and slender, with a dark fretboard and dot inlays, leading up to a headstock with six inline tuning pegs. The back of the guitar is solid black, and the hardware appears to be metallic, likely chrome or nickel-plated. The overall design is sleek and modern, suitable for rock or metal genres, and would stand out in an urban street performance or music venue.
description: A black V-shaped electric guitar with a cream pickguard, two humbucker pickups, and a sleek, modern design. It features a long neck with dot inlays and a headstock with six inline tuners.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the guitar's thin body and neck, with minimal visible hardware.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the V-shaped body, cream pickguard, two humbucker pickups, control knobs, and the full neck with headstock.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, again highlighting the slimness of the guitar and the straight alignment of the neck.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the solid black back of the guitar, the V-shaped body, and the headstock with six tuning pegs.
attribute_headstock_design: Six inline tuners, pointed headstock, no visible logo
